title: Australian Outback Geology
extract: The Australian Outback encompasses approximately 5.6 million square kilometers of arid and semi-arid terrain, representing one of Earth's most geologically diverse landscapes. The region features formations spanning over 3.5 billion years of geological history, including the Pilbara Craton's ancient stromatolites—among the earliest evidence of life on Earth. Uluru, a massive sandstone monolith rising 348 meters above the surrounding plain, demonstrates the erosion-resistant properties of arkose sandstone. The geological stability of the Australian continental plate, combined with minimal tectonic activity, has preserved mineral deposits of exceptional economic significance, including iron ore, gold, and rare earth elements.
